summary: Czech nationalist composer, most commonly identified by the tone poem cycle Ma vlast (which includes The Moldau depicting the Vltava River) and the opera The Bartered Bride. Went deaf in 1874. Wrote the autobiographical string quartet From My Life. Associated with Prague and the Prague Spring Festival. Often clued alongside Dvorak.

works.name: Ma vlast (My Country / My Fatherland)
works.description: Cycle of six symphonic/tone poems depicting Bohemia. The most commonly referenced movements are Vltava (The Moldau) and Vysehrad.

works.name: String Quartet No. 1 in E minor ("From My Life")
works.description: Autobiographical string quartet. Smetana's most commonly clued chamber work.

works.name: The Bartered Bride (Prodana nevesta)
works.description: Comic opera, Smetana's best-known operatic work.

works.name: General / Biographical
works.description: Clues about Smetana himself, not tied to a specific work.

works.name: Other Operas (Dalibor, The Brandenburgers in Bohemia)

recursive_suggestions: The Moldau (Vltava) — heavily clued tone poem, likely has its own answerline
From My Life (String Quartet No. 1) — appeared as its own answerline at DECAF 2022
The Bartered Bride — appeared as its own answerline
Vysehrad — the opening tone poem of Ma vlast
